read: The source-indexed selected-limit kernel removes the packed probability/logit slab and is faster than pack+kernel, but the selected-limit route plan is still too expensive at S4096 when built as host metadata.
The plan-free group-filter TileLang kernel avoids route planning entirely and reduces compressed dKV inside group-panel CTAs before global atomics.
At S4096, group-filter is much better than the previous selected-limit bridge wall path, but it is still about 1 ms slower than explicit PyTorch for the current materialized bridge.

The remaining high-return path is not another Python route-plan tweak. It is fusing or adjacent-producing P/dP from Q/K/V/LSE and LI derivative so the bridge stops materializing selected-KV/probs/grad_logits, while keeping selected-limit-style reduced writes if route metadata can be produced cheaply on device.
next: Keep explicit_pytorch as the stable default and explicit_tilelang_dkv as the plan-free TileLang candidate gate.
Use group_filter for wall-time experiments when route planning would dominate.
Return to selected-limit scheduling only when LI route metadata is already available on device or fused into the producer.
